yesterdayStoryline.headline: Germany score seven, Japan salvage late point
yesterdayStoryline.subtitle: Group E opened with German authority; Group F already looks more congested.

todayStoryline.headline: Spain start Group H, Belgium open Group G
todayStoryline.subtitle: Four fixtures stretch from Atlanta to Los Angeles, with Uruguay favoured in Miami.

yesterday.scorers: Felix Nmecha 6' (GER), Nico Schlotterbeck 38' (GER), Kai Havertz 45+5' pen, 88' (GER), Jamal Musiala 47' (GER), Nathaniel Brown 68' (GER), Denis Undav 78' (GER) · Livano Comenencia 21' (CUW)

lines: Germany’s front line found balance after Curaçao briefly levelled through a historic first World Cup goal.
The margin puts immediate pressure on Ivory Coast and Ecuador in Group E.

yesterday.scorers: Virgil van Dijk 51' (NED), Crysencio Summerville 64' (NED) · Keito Nakamura 57' (JPN), Daichi Kamada 89' (JPN)

lines: The match stayed flat before four second-half goals turned it into a late exchange.
Japan’s point keeps Group F open after the Netherlands failed to manage their lead.
Ecuador struck the woodwork twice early before Ivory Coast improved after attacking changes.
Amad’s late winner gives Ivory Coast a useful Group E platform before Germany.

yesterday.scorers: Yasin Ayari 7', 90+6' (SWE), Alexander Isak 30' (SWE), Viktor Gyökeres 59' (SWE), Mattias Svanberg 84' (SWE) · Omar Rekik 43' (TUN)

lines: Sweden’s forwards combined cleanly and Ayari’s long-range finishing stretched Tunisia throughout.
The five-goal win sends Sweden top of Group F after one round.

facts.home: Marc Cucurella dyed his famous curls red after promising it for Spain’s Euro win.
facts.away: Cape Verde boss Bubista’s nickname simply comes from Boa Vista, his home island.

facts.home: Kevin De Bruyne once compared baking a cake to crafting the perfect pass.
facts.away: Salah once joked his PlayStation version is stronger than the real Mohamed Salah.

facts.home: Hervé Renard’s lucky white shirt became almost as famous as Saudi Arabia’s upset.
facts.away: Luis Suárez’s statue in Salto once ‘left’ a note for Messi’s wedding.

facts.home: Iran’s Team Melli nickname is wonderfully literal: it simply means ‘the national team’.
facts.away: New Zealand’s 1982 qualifiers involved 15 matches and about 55,000 travel miles.

news.headline: Britain announces sweeping social media ban for under-16s
news.body: Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Britain would ban under-16s from social media sites and add restrictions on gaming and livestreaming platforms. The government framed the move as a child-safety measure, while the policy places Britain among the countries tightening rules on young people’s online access.
